verification.note: The Section 80C Rs 1,50,000 ceiling, the Section 80D health-insurance limits (Rs 25,000 when everyone insured is below 60, Rs 50,000 when a senior citizen is covered), and the extra Section 80CCD(1B) NPS Rs 50,000 are long-standing, well-known limits carried unchanged into FY 2025-26. They apply only under the OLD regime; the new regime allows none of them. Cross-checked against multiple current filing guides. Confirm against the official instructions on incometax.gov.in before relying on this as filing advice.

section_80c.covers: EPF/PPF, ELSS, life-insurance premiums, home-loan principal, tax-saving fixed deposits, children's tuition fees, and similar.

section_80d.covers: Health-insurance premiums (plus limited preventive check-up costs) for you and your family.

section_80ccd_1b_nps.covers: An extra deduction for your own National Pension System (NPS) contribution, over and above the Section 80C limit.
